title: Server-side requests should not be vulnerable to forging attacks
description: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) occurs when attackers can coerce a server to perform arbitrary requests on their behalf. An SSRF vulnerability can either be basic or blind, depending on whether the server’s fetched data is directly returned in the web application’s response. The absence of the corresponding response for the coerced request on the application is not a barrier to exploitation and thus must be treated in the same way as basic SSRF.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: HTTP client call with user-controlled URL without validation

conditions: HTTP client method call (GetAsync, GetStringAsync, PostAsync, SendAsync)
URL parameter is user input (method parameter or variable named url/uri/path/endpoint)
No URL validation visible in preceding code (allowlist, Uri validation, domain check)

logic.reasoning: SSRF allows attackers to make server perform requests to arbitrary URLs, accessing internal resources or exfiltrating data. User input in HTTP client calls without validation is dangerous. Uses custom function to track method parameters and identify unvalidated URL usage in HTTP calls.

should_not_trigger:
if (!allowedDomains.Contains(uri.Host)) throw new Exception();
await client.GetStringAsync(url);
